purpose: Test whether cross-file process artifacts can be encoded into substrate vector space and decoded back to the same outputs faster than the classical baseline.
Test whether startup telemetry can drive the same substrate path while accounting for noise, resonant nodes, and kernel actuation latency.
Test whether a real Vulkan compute dispatch can actuate the live telemetry capture path strongly enough to form an actuation-adjusted telemetry plane for the calculus.
supported_claims: Substrate microprocessing reproduced the same mixed-artifact output as the classical baseline.
Synthetic startup telemetry reproduced both result and trace-state equality between substrate and classical paths.

A real Vulkan-backed live startup capture also reproduced both result and trace-state equality between substrate and classical paths after projecting the kernel actuation metadata into the working telemetry plane.
The bounded substrate path was faster than the classical comparison path in all three tested scenarios.
limitations: The live telemetry source is aggregate device telemetry, not raw per-kernel counters.
The short-burst live run required actuation-adjusted telemetry because the coarse OS raw_gpu_util counter stayed at zero.
The measured speedups are runtime-path speedups and are not proof of native Vulkan or CUDA acceleration for the whole engine.
The live equivalence result applies to the same frozen live frame window, not to continuously changing live telemetry.
application_implications: Engine routing, mixed text/structure/calculation preprocessing, and telemetry-aware control can be studied as substrate microprocess workloads.
Crypto mining can adopt the same pattern for job preprocessing, candidate ranking, and submission packet preparation while classical audit remains the external guard.
Kernel actuation scheduling can use resonant-node and latency forecasts before the actuation point instead of only reacting after the fact.
A future mining implementation can treat real pulse-kernel actuation as the source event and use the runtime only to decode and packetize the emergent result.
